question.content: For an infinitely long straight wire with uniform linear charge density lambda, write the electric-field magnitude E at a point P located distance r from the wire in vacuum. Use epsilon_0 for the vacuum permittivity. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Preserve the ASCII notation style and variable naming used in the problem; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Set cylindrical symmetry for an infinite line charge and use radial distance r from wire to field point P.
step_1.key_point: Field is radial and depends only on r.

step_3.content: Take a charge element lambda*dz at coordinate z and project its contribution along radial direction.
step_3.key_point: Use d and theta to express the projection factor.

step_5.content: Apply substitution d=r/cos(theta), z=r*tan(theta), integrate theta from -pi/2 to pi/2, then use E=-dV/dr.
step_5.key_point: Obtain E(r) then integrate radially for V.

step_7.content: Integrate the radial field between reference radius r0 and radius r to get logarithmic potential form.
step_7.key_point: Potential of infinite line uses finite reference radius instead of infinity.
